Several household items, including furniture and mirrors, are said to be haunted by the spirits of people who died under tragic circumstances. 
These items, often referred to as "haunted" or "cursed," are believed to be responsible for a range of paranormal occurrences.

A mother and her five-year-old daughter survived a car accident in Melbourne that killed three of their children, including two four-year-old twins and a one-year-old boy.
The mother, Akon Guode, was driving the 2005 Toyota Kluger when it plunged into Lake Gladman, and her daughter Aluel Manyang believes the children were eaten by crocodiles.

A group of 10 doctors criticized Dr. Mehmet Oz for promoting unproven treatments and products on his show, but he successfully defended himself by pointing out the questionable credentials of his critics, including ties to the GMO industry.
Oz's producers responded with an investigative segment that exposed the critics' motives and highlighted their own flaws, boosting his media empire.
The incident has raised questions about the balance between Dr. Oz's free speech and the potential harm caused by his unproven

Google has improved its handwriting recognition on Android phones to understand 82 languages in 20 distinct scripts.
The update allows users to input text using handwriting, cursive, or emoji, and complements typing and speech input methods.
Google has also launched a new feature to locate lost phones using the "find my phone" tool, which works with all Android phones with the latest Google app installed.
The tool can ring the phone, show its location on a map, and even remotely call

A massive secondary magma chamber beneath Yellowstone National Park has been discovered, measuring 11,035 cubic miles and containing 98% hot rock.
The chamber is 12-28 miles beneath the supervolcano and is believed to have fueled three eruptions 2 million, 1.2 million, and 640,000 years ago.
Its discovery explains the high levels of carbon dioxide released by the supervolcano and could provide clues about the structure of other supervolcanoes

At 80, Jan Bearman got her first tattoo in memory of her daughter Shell, who died of complications from diabetes in 2011.
Jan has since gotten a new tattoo every birthday to commemorate her daughter, with her latest design featuring a colored shell below Shell's name.
Jan's decision to get tattoos was met with surprise from her family, but they were supportive of her decision to honor her daughter's memory.
Jan now has three tattoos, and is unsure if she will

A 12-year-old King Charles Spaniel named Alfie suffered third-degree burns from a heated pad used during an MRI scan at the Animal Health Trust clinic in Newmarket, Suffolk.
The pad was warmed in a microwave, but it's unclear how it was left unattended and applied to Alfie's back without being noticed.
The clinic has since changed its practice and now uses an incubator to heat the pads, and Alfie is receiving treatment for his burns, which may

Kim Kardashian is creating an 'archive' of her designer wardrobe, planning to hand down everything to her 22-month-old daughter North West.
The reality star stores her most infamous outfits in clear plastic bags, including her Vogue cover dress and Met Gala gowns.
Kim wants North to follow in her footsteps and be confident in her style, encouraging her daughter to be herself and take risks.

Bulgaria has been named Europe's cheapest destination, with a total price of £37.39 for a 10-item shopping basket, down 13.6% from last summer.
The country's Black Sea resorts, such as Sunny Beach, offer the best value in terms of a meal out and other holiday activities.
Only Turkey, with a basket price of £65.70, is more expensive, due to higher inflation rates.

A 61-year-old Florida mailman, Doug Hughes, landed his gyro-copter on the US Capitol lawn, breaching the no-fly zone, and was apprehended by Secret Service officers with guns drawn.
Hughes claimed he was delivering letters to Congress to protest campaign finance reform and government corruption.
The incident raised concerns about security and the potential for harm, but Hughes said he had no violent inclinations and intended to draw attention to the issue.
Hughes faces charges of unlawful

A sheep-herding dog named Stains was given a lion-like makeover by its groomers.
The dog's owner was initially surprised by the result, but laughed hysterically when he saw the transformation.
Stains now has a shaved coat with a voluminous mane around its head and neck, giving it a lion-like appearance.
The dog's owner continues to laugh and the other dogs in the house seem to appreciate the new look.

Motherwell will challenge the SPFL over free entry to Premiership play-offs for season-ticket holders if they finish 11th.
The club will offer free tickets to their season-ticket holders if they reach the play-offs.
Rangers and Hibernian are set to back Motherwell in their dispute with the SPFL.

Rory McIlroy and Niall Horan, a One Direction singer, caddied for each other at the Masters Par-3 Contest.
As part of a deal, McIlroy will sing with Horan on stage at Gillette Stadium in Massachusetts on September 12.
The two will perform together at the Deutsche Bank Championship in Boston, which McIlroy is also competing in.
Horan has joked that McIlroy's singing voice is "terrible".

Grenada is renowned for its fresh produce and local recipes, with an abundance of exotic fruits and spices available.
The island offers a range of culinary experiences, from cooking demonstrations to visiting local markets and restaurants.
One of the national dishes, oil down, is a hearty, one-pot meal with salted meat, chicken, and dumplings stewed in coconut milk.
The Eshima Ohashi bridge in Japan is a 1.7km long, 1-mile wide bridge with a steep slope, making it a challenging hill start for drivers.

A Washington teen, Ibrahim Ahmad, was suspended from school for 5 days after wearing a vest with fake explosives to propose to his date at prom.
Ahmad claims the decision was unfair and felt racist, as he is Middle Eastern and others may have gotten away with similar pranks.
The prank was meant to be a creative and funny way to ask his date, Rilea Wolfe, to prom, but it ultimately ruined the event for both of them.
Ahmad's date

University of South Carolina student suspended after being pictured writing 'n*****s' on a whiteboard as reason for poor WiFi.
The student, a former member of the Kappa Delta Chapter sorority, is also facing a code of conduct inquiry.
The incident marks the third racially-charged incident on a college campus in the past week.
University president Harris Pastides condemned the student's actions, stating that racist and uncivil rhetoric have no place at the university.

Burnley's Ashley Barnes was sent off for a 'criminal challenge' on Nemanja Matic in February, but was given a second chance when Chelsea's Jose Mourinho condemned the tackle.
Barnes was denied a penalty for a challenge on John Stones, and allowed to stay on the pitch for a high tackle on George Boyd.

Patricia Ebel, 49, was arrested on Friday after crashing her luxury black BMW into a stopped car in Naples, Florida, with her 10-year-old grandson in the vehicle.
Ebel failed sobriety tests and had a blood alcohol content of.15 or greater, refusing a breathalyzer test.
Neither Ebel nor her grandson was injured in the crash, but Ebel's car was totaled, and the driver of the other vehicle was taken to the hospital with

An 11-year-old boy, Shiva, was beaten to death by his school head teacher, Lalit Verma, for allegedly stealing a pencil and eraser from another boy's bag.
Verma was arrested and charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder after Shiva died from internal bleeding.
This incident is not an isolated case, with reports of other Indian boys being beaten to death by teachers for various reasons.

Queen Margrethe celebrated her 75th birthday with a two-day celebration, including a gala banquet at Fredensborg Castle and a family wake-up call at Amalienborg Palace.
The Danish royal family, including Crown Prince Frederik, Crown Princess Mary, and their children, joined other European royals for the festivities.
The celebrations included a procession down Copenhagen's shopping street Strøget and a dinner at Christiansborg Palace, where speeches were made, including a tribute from Crown

Everybody Loves Raymond actor Sawyer Sweeten, 19, took his own life while visiting a family member's home in Texas.
His manager said there were no signs of depression or suicidal warning signs prior to the incident.
The child star, who played Geoffrey Barone on the show, was described as a "good kid" and "very sweet" by his co-stars and family members.
His sister Madylin wrote on Facebook, encouraging people to reach out to loved ones and expressing

The average British worker will attend 6,239 meetings in their career, with 60% finding most meetings "pretty pointless". One in five workers admit to nodding off during meetings, with 70% constantly zoning out. Meetings are often seen as a waste of time, with 45% feeling their company wastes money on travel and 37% fiddling expenses.

Lewis Hamilton discussed the meaning behind his tattoos in an interview with Men's Health magazine.
The two-time Formula One champion has a range of tattoos, including religious images, musical notes, and inspirational quotes.
Hamilton believes the physical demands of racing are often under-estimated by fans, citing intense training and physical demands of the sport.
An amateur astronomer captured a photo of a sun spot that resembles a chick sitting on the sun's surface, which is actually a common phenomenon caused by a concentration of magnetic fields.

Photographer James Oatway captured a brutal attack on Mozambican Emmanuel Sithole in Johannesburg's Alexandra Township.
Sithole was bludgeoned and stabbed to death in broad daylight, and Oatway's photos showed the intensity of the attack.
The violence is part of a larger wave of xenophobic attacks in South Africa, with seven people killed and many more displaced.
The UN and South African leaders have begun to take notice of the attacks, with President Jacob Zuma

Demolition of Archway Sheet Metal Works' site begins, clearing the way for Tottenham's new stadium.
The demolition marks the final hurdle for Spurs' £400m redevelopment of White Hart Lane.
Archway had challenged the compulsory purchase order, but lost the appeal and failed to appeal to the Court of Appeal.
Tottenham plan to open their new 56,000-seater stadium in 2018, with a central square, shops, and housing included in the project.

Certain types of men can be bad bets for women, despite their charm and charisma.
To spot a potentially toxic partner, ask questions about their emotional maturity, affection, honesty, adulting skills, self-esteem, and overall positivity.
If they struggle with these areas, it may indicate a higher risk of heartbreak and relationship problems.
A healthy relationship requires mutual respect, trust, and emotional intelligence.

A bear in Alaska's Katmai National Park was unable to catch a salmon that leaped straight into its mouth.
The bear, waiting at a waterfall, was outsmarted by the fish, which continued its journey upstream.
The salmon, a Sockeye, was in a prime position to be caught, but the bear made a crucial error of judgment, allowing it to escape.
The incident was captured by photographer Juergen Sohns, who said the bear was waiting

ABC is filming a proof of concept for a revival of "The Muppets" with "The Big Bang Theory" co-creator Bill Prady co-writing the script.
The pilot presentation, if successful, could go straight to series and feature the original Muppet characters gathering at ABC Studios.
The show's production is being led by Bob Kushell and will include celebrity cameos and new roles, but Miss Piggy's current relationship with Kermit is a major hurdle to the

Gerry Adams sparked outrage with comments on US TV suggesting that abduction and murder of Jean McConville were 'what happens in wars'.
He claimed that such incidents occur in every conflict, sparking anger from McConville's family.
Adams' comments were seen as insensitive and dismissive of the family's suffering, with his daughter-in-law Helen McKendry calling him a 'liar'.
Adams has denied any involvement in McConville's murder, but his

Crystal Palace manager Alan Pardew denies plans to sign Newcastle goalkeeper Tim Krul.
Pardew is focused on strengthening the team in the summer transfer market.
He is happy with current goalkeeper Julian Speroni and has no contact with Newcastle or Krul.
Pardew wants to take the next step for the club, whether through buying or selling players.

The Queen is hiring a sous chef to develop their technical expertise using fresh ingredients from her Royal gardens and estates.
The successful applicant will work at Buckingham Palace and travel to other Royal residences for three months a year.
The job pays around £28,000-a-year and includes accommodation, meals, and a comprehensive benefits package.
The Queen's former personal chef revealed that she enjoys simple foods like Special K, jam sandwiches, and chocolate cake.

The grave of Lizzie Borden and her family in Fall River, Massachusetts was vandalized with black and green paint over the weekend.
The vandalism was discovered on Monday, the day after a Lifetime miniseries about Borden premiered on Sunday night.
The incident is not the first time the plot has been vandalized, with the grave previously being covered in various items such as pennies, flowers, and notes.

Young Iranians are defying the country's hardline Islamic image by living openly gay lifestyles, drinking alcohol, and engaging in Westernized activities such as kissing in public and wearing Western-style clothing. The Iranian youth is among the most politically active groups in the country, with over 63% of the population under 30, and has influenced the country's changing relationship with the US. A couple in Oregon, Dale and Debra Krein, were awarded $240,000 compensation for over a decade of disquiet caused by their neighbors' Tibetan mastiffs barking until 5am.
The dogs' barking was deemed a public nuisance, and a jury ruled that the canines should be debarked within 60 days.
The defendants, John Updegraff and Karen Szewc, had attempted to argue that the mastiffs were needed to protect their

Driver followed sat nav down country lane and lost roof of lorry.
Roof was suspended from a tree behind the vehicle, leaving it "hung out to dry".
Police tweeted a picture to warn other drivers of the dangers of following sat nav.
Driver may face charges for reckless driving.

Rev. Robert H. Schuller, a Southern California televangelist, died at 88 after a long battle with cancer.
Schuller's ministry, which included the Crystal Cathedral, collapsed in recent years due to a leadership transition and financial struggles.
He was known for his positive and inclusive message, which blended Bible and psychology, and had admirers including Billy Graham and several US presidents.
Schuller's empire crumbled after his son, Robert A., was installed

The bodies of two people were found amid the wreckage of a light aircraft that crashed near Loch Etive in western Scotland.
Police Scotland launched a search and rescue operation after the plane lost contact with ground control near Beinn Nan Lus, a boggy hill in Argyll.
The plane was travelling from central Scotland to Tiree, the most westerly island of the inner Hebrides, when it went missing.
A report will be submitted to the Procurator Fiscal

Indiana has seen a significant increase in HIV cases, with 120 confirmed cases and 10 preliminary positive cases in Scott County, a 30-mile radius north of Louisville, Kentucky.
The state's health officials have said they expect the number of cases to rise as more people are tested, putting pressure on Governor Mike Pence to extend the 30-day needle exchange program.
The program, approved by Pence in March, has provided 5,322 clean syringes to 86 participants

Reading goalkeeper Adam Federici had already booked his wedding for the weekend of the FA Cup final, which Reading lost to Arsenal at Wembley.
Federici's error led to Arsenal's 2-1 victory, but he is expected to bounce back from the loss. Federici is set to marry his girlfriend Micaela Gardner in Florence, Italy on the same weekend as the FA Cup final. The goalkeeper has apologized to fans for his mistake and is backed by his team

The NCAA claims it has no legal responsibility to ensure the academic integrity of courses offered to student-athletes.
The organization's stance contradicts its previous rhetoric and defense of amateurism, which claims athletes are paid with an education.
Critics argue the NCAA's hands-off approach undermines the education of athletes and ignores the academic fraud scandal at UNC.
The NCAA's actions have led to calls for the organization's dissolution, with some questioning its purpose and tax-exempt status.

Sanjay Chaddah, 43, was given a six-month suspended sentence for common assault and perjury after falsely accusing his neighbour's son-in-law of a racist assault.
Chaddah had concocted a "tissue of lies" claiming Dean Paton had kicked him and used a racist slur, but CCTV footage cleared Paton of any wrongdoing.
The dispute began over a new driveway and wall, with Chaddah's neighbours, Steve and Tricia Boden

Sebastiano Quagliata and his wife Valbona Lucaj pleaded no contest to owning a dangerous dog causing death in the fatal mauling of Craig Sytsma in Michigan.
They agreed to 15 years in jail and could be deported after serving their sentences.
The couple's dogs had attacked at least two neighbors before killing Sytsma, and some neighbors had taken matters into their own hands by arming themselves to protect themselves.
The sentencing will be in June,

Cesc Fabregas scored a late winner for Chelsea in a 1-0 victory over QPR at Loftus Road, moving Jose Mourinho's side seven points clear at the top of the table.
The win was a vital one for Chelsea, who had to work hard to secure the three points against a resolute QPR defence.
Fabregas, who wore a protective mask after breaking his nose last week, admitted the goal was "vital" and praised the team

Chelsea will make a late decision on whether to risk Diego Costa against Stoke due to a hamstring injury.
Stoke forward Jonathan Walters is expected to shake off a calf knock and be available for the match.
Chelsea have won 10 and lost just one of the 13 Premier League meetings with Stoke City.
Chelsea have kept 16 clean sheets in their last 22 Premier League matches at Stamford Bridge.

Toby Alderweireld will return to Atletico Madrid at the end of his season-long loan at Southampton.
The Belgian defender has impressed this season and has been linked to permanent moves to Southampton and Tottenham.
Atletico's sporting director Jose Luis Perez Caminero says the club is counting on Alderweireld's return to La Liga.

Tottenham will subsidise Emmanuel Adebayor's wages to make him available for loan or transfer this summer.
The striker's £100,000-per-week wages will be partially covered by the club to facilitate his departure.
Adebayor has made just nine league starts this season and is unlikely to regain a regular spot under Mauricio Pochettino.
The 31-year-old Togolese forward is content with life off the pitch and has expressed pride in his

Celebrity chef Pete Evans' reworked baby milk formula has been criticized by dietitians for containing excessive vitamin A, which could harm babies.
The formula, 'Happy Tummy Brew', was reworked to reduce the vitamin A content, but experts say it still poses a risk to babies.
Evans has been promoting the Paleo diet, which he claims has helped alleviate symptoms of multiple sclerosis in some people, including a woman who shared her story on his Facebook page.
However,

A study found that the Fisher-Price magnets from the 1970s and 1980s may have caused a generation to associate certain letters with specific colors, with 15% of participants born between 1970 and 1985 having learned these associations.
The magnets' colors were found to match the most commonly chosen colors for letters among synesthetes, with A linked to red, F to purple, and P to green.

A typed surrender order issued by German president Karl Doenitz on May 8, 1945, is being auctioned for £21,000. The order, which was found on Luftwaffe chief Robert von Greim when he was arrested, instructed officers to abandon fighting or face the Allies' wrath. Doenitz had signed the instrument of surrender just a day earlier, bringing an end to war in Europe. The note is one of the rare copies of Doen

Juan Mata has turned his season around under Louis van Gaal, scoring crucial goals and inspiring Manchester United to victories.
Mata's form has been boosted by a change in United's playing style, which allows him to thrive in a more balanced team.
The midfielder has also found a new sense of freedom and happiness at United, away from the constraints of Chelsea under Jose Mourinho.

BMW Mini has produced concept smart specs, called Augmented Vision goggles, that give drivers super-human powers such as X-ray-like vision and virtual displays for directions and other features.
The goggles can project navigation information, speed limits, and local landmarks onto the road, and even show external objects concealed by the car.
The idea is to show relevant information in the driver's direct field of view without obscuring other road users, boosting safety and convenience.
The goggles are connected to a smartphone

A former paramedic, Crsity Collins, has spoken out about her addiction to ice while working and treating patients, highlighting the lack of support available to those struggling with the drug. Collins, who was using ice daily and driving while high, said she was never offered support or a psych assessment despite checking herself into the emergency room multiple times. She has now been clean for nine months after seeking help from the Department of Veterans Affairs and is using her story to raise awareness about the

Women throughout history have used a variety of unusual methods to beautify themselves, including Cleopatra's donkey-milk baths and Egyptian queen Nefertiti's homemade cosmetics made from lead-based kohl and crushed beetles.
In ancient Greece, women used honey and olive oil to achieve a pale complexion, while in ancient India, they used cow dung and urine to boost their beauty routine.
Other unusual beauty practices from ancient times include the use of rice flour to whiten faces

Israeli developers have created a 'thermal protection' case called Amited that warns and cools down phones when they get too hot.
The case uses micro-fans to dissipate heat and resistance coils to heat the phone if it gets too cold.
It syncs with a smartphone over Bluetooth and tracks temperature changes, allowing users to set custom operation modes and alarm thresholds.
Amited aims to prevent overheating and battery damage by keeping phones at an optimal temperature range of 0-35

Three men in their 50s have been arrested on suspicion of murdering missing chef Claudia Lawrence.
The arrests are linked to new lines of inquiry into her disappearance, which began in 2013.
Detectives are still searching for evidence and are treating her disappearance as a suspected murder.
The investigation has been ongoing for five years, with the family and police continuing to seek answers.

Nursultan Nazarbayev has won re-election as Kazakhstan's president with 97% of the vote, citing the need to avoid an "undemocratic" outcome. International monitors have raised concerns about the election's legitimacy due to limited opposition candidates. Nazarbayev has promoted market reforms and attracted significant foreign investment, but the economy is forecast to slow due to declining energy prices.

Stephen James Hendry, 24, is a full-tattooed English model who has become one of the most in-demand models in the industry.
He has lucrative contracts with Calvin Klein and Diesel and has starred in numerous men's magazine shoots.
Despite his tough exterior, Hendry is humble and says his tattoos are about expression, art, and staying grounded.
He is currently in Australia for a new campaign with Windsor Smith shoes and is excited to take his newfound fame in stride

Danielle and Alexander Meitiv, Maryland parents who practice "free-range" parenting, are suing authorities after their 10-year-old son and 6-year-old daughter were picked up by police and held for nearly six hours without food. The children were initially taken by police after a concerned citizen reported seeing them unaccompanied near a park, and were later turned over to Child Protective Services. Aritz Aduriz scored a late equaliser for Athletic Bilbao to deny Valencia victory and secure a 1-1 draw.
The result sees Valencia move above Sevilla into fourth place in the pursuit of Champions League qualification.
Getafe moved 10 points clear of the bottom three with a 1-0 win over Elche.

Italy's navy boarded and retook control of a fishing boat seized by gunmen off the coast of Sicily.
The boat, carrying 7 people, including 3 Sicilians and 4 Tunisians, was intercepted 90 km from the Libyan port of Misrata.
Rifle shots were accidentally fired during the operation, injuring one of the fishermen.
The incident highlights the dangers faced by migrants making the sea journey from North Africa to Europe.

Jason McDonald, 34, from Colorado, walked a slackline over a pool of 50 hungry alligators at the Colorado Gator Farm.
He had been handling alligators for over 10 years and thought this would be an interesting new way to interact with them.
The alligators surprisingly didn't seem to bother him as he walked over the slackline.
McDonald plans to take his hobby further, including walking over the farm's largest alligators and albino gators.

Twitter suspended over 10,000 accounts linked to Islamic State militants and their supporters in a 24-hour period.
The accounts were suspended due to violent threats and sickening messages promoting violence, following a massive campaign by online activists to expose and report ISIS-affiliated accounts.
The social network remains committed to free speech, but will not tolerate accounts spreading hate and violence, and experts say it's an ongoing challenge to keep up with the proliferation of pro-ISIS content.

Jade Wimsey, 24, from Yorkshire, spent £11,000 on energy drinks over four years, consuming up to seven cans a day, and ballooned to a size 20. She lost six stone in 18 months after quitting energy drinks and adopting a healthy diet. Graeme Finlay, 53, accused of attacking elderly couple on luxury cruise ship
He claims violence erupted over awkward silence at dinner and self-defence after being attacked
Finlay denies charges of unlawful wounding and assault causing grievous bodily harm

Two brothers, Jeff and Joey Stallings, were hospitalized after taking a 'bad batch' of synthetic marijuana, also known as Spice, in Mississippi.
The brothers, both in their twenties, fell seriously ill with hallucinations, vomiting, night sweats, and violent shaking, and were placed on life support.
Fortunately, they recovered and were discharged from hospital, but their mother warns that the brothers are now addicted to synthetic marijuana and face serious medical issues, including permanent kidney damage.

Liverpool manager Brendan Rodgers is set to include Jordon Ibe in his squad for the match against Newcastle on Monday after the 19-year-old's impressive training sessions.
Ibe had damaged his knee ligaments during a Europa League tie against Besiktas but has made a swift recovery.
The young England international had previously impressed for Liverpool with a Man-of-the-Match performance in the Merseyside derby.

Colin Heath, 43, was jailed for three years and two months for manslaughter after admitting to violently shaking seven-month-old Kealeigh-Anne Woolley in 2000.
The child suffered severe brain injuries and was left permanently disabled, spending most of her life in a wheelchair and requiring a body cast.

Robert Dellinger, 54, was sentenced to 20 years in prison for causing a highway crash that killed a Vermont couple and their unborn baby in December 2013.
Dellinger pleaded guilty to negligent homicide and assault for the deaths of Amanda Murphy, 24, and Jason Timmons, 29, and their unborn baby.
The crash occurred when Dellinger's truck crossed the median and sheared off the top of the couple's SUV, killing them instantly.
D

The native Australian bilby is under threat due to low numbers, with only small populations remaining in Queensland, the Northern Territory, and Western Australia.
Predators like feral cats and foxes, as well as habitat destruction and competition with introduced species, are major threats to the bilby's survival.
A captive breeding program is being developed to help save the endangered marsupial, with the goal of increasing its population in controlled environments.

Builders in Turkey have discovered a massive underground city in Cappadocia, consisting of 3.5 miles of tunnels, secret churches, tombs, and safe havens.
The subterranean city, dating back around 5,000 years, is similar to the famous Derinkuyu underground city, but is larger, with early estimates suggesting it could be around a third bigger.
The city was built to protect residents from invaders, but remains largely unexplored,

Anthony Barbour, 33, from Liverpool, takes 360-degree photographs of British locations like parks, villages, and beaches.
He spends hours slowly rotating in one spot, taking 50 pictures to create a single image, which he then edits together using Photoshop.
Barbour uses this technique as an escape from a recently diagnosed illness, Ulcerative Pan Colitis, and to keep himself artistically occupied.

A fireball that exploded over Russia last year shared its orbit with a large asteroid, 2014 UR116, which is due to pass by the moon in 2017.
Researchers used camera footage to recreate the fireball's trajectory and found it was similar to the orbit of the Annama meteorite, a 1,100lb rock.
The study suggests that streams of asteroid fragments can travel on nearly identical orbits, and that tidal forces may disperse debris over time.

A bomb technician created beeping Easter eggs for blind children to hunt, using a transmitter that lets off a high-pitched sound.
The device was developed by David Hyche nine years ago for his blind daughter, who wanted to participate in Easter egg hunts.
The beeping eggs were used in an Easter egg hunt at the Alabama Institute for Deaf and Blind in Birmingham, where blind children filled baskets with the eggs and traded them in for candy.

An 8-year-old girl, Marie Surprenant, wrote a heartfelt letter to her social workers and detectives thanking them for helping her escape an abusive home and finding her a loving adoptive family.
She was severely injured as an infant and permanently lost the use of her legs, but is now thriving in her new home with her adoptive mother Michele.

Freddie Gray, 25, died one week after being taken into police custody in Baltimore, with the cause of death still unclear.
Attorney for Gray's family alleges police are involved in a cover-up, claiming Gray was dragged and thrown into a police van without probable cause.
Gray's spinal cord was severely injured, and his family is demanding answers about the circumstances of his death.
Police are declining to release details, citing ongoing investigations, but have placed the officers involved on administrative

Jameela Jamil, a 29-year-old TV presenter, had all her amalgam fillings removed after experiencing severe health problems, including swelling, headaches, and fatigue, which she believes were caused by the mercury in the fillings.
She spent £3,000 on the procedure, which she hoped would alleviate her symptoms, but they continued to affect her for several years.

Cities around the world have unique nicknames that reflect their history, environment, and industry.
For example, Las Vegas is known as "Sin City" due to a building used by prostitutes in the early 1900s.
Some nicknames, like Paris being "The City of Love", come from romantic architecture and landmarks, while others, like Amsterdam being "The Venice of the North", come from its canals.

Richie Benaud, a renowned Australian cricketer and television commentator, has died at the age of 84 after a battle with skin cancer.
Benaud was a versatile sportsman who won 63 Test caps and took 248 wickets, but is best remembered for his dry wit and style as a commentator.
He was a pioneer in bringing a more human and emotional approach to sports broadcasting, and his influence can still be seen in modern commentators.

Michelle Knight, who was held captive for almost 10 years, said she refuses to be considered a victim and instead wants to "overcome and flourish" from her experiences.
Knight, who appeared at the Cleveland Rape Crisis Center's Faces of Change Luncheon, revealed she has a boyfriend and is working on a second book about her life since being rescued.
She has also started a new relationship with music, recording a song called "Survivor" and learning to play the piano.

More than 200 Yazidi prisoners have been released from Islamic State captivity in northern Iraq after nearly a year.
The freed prisoners, including women, children, and the elderly, are in poor health and bearing signs of abuse and neglect.
No reason was given for the release, but it is believed to be a surprise move by the group.
About 40 children were among those released, and many Yazidi community leaders were present to receive them.

A speed camera in Birmingham has been angled to point at a house's front door after not working for two years.
The camera was part of 300 speed and traffic cameras turned off in the West Midlands in 2013 due to outdated technology.
A council engineer will visit the site to assess the situation and potentially remove the camera.
The camera will not be included in a planned pilot using digital technology.

Paul Gregory, 51, of Bedford, was fined £795 for misrepresenting his physical abilities to obtain a blue badge. He claimed he could only walk 200 meters, but was actually a keen walker and had completed a 191-mile hike. Council officials discovered his true abilities after reading a newspaper article about his hiking exploits. Gregory was also ordered to carry out 150 hours of unpaid work and undergo 'thinking skills' sessions.
